frameworks.name: machine-learning classification pipeline
frameworks.one_line: feature extraction plus selection plus classifier applied to EEG for emotion labels

frameworks.name: empirical mode decomposition / intrinsic mode functions
frameworks.one_line: EMD/IMF used as the signal-decomposition stage for EEG feature extraction

frameworks.name: variational mode decomposition
frameworks.one_line: VMD used alongside EMD/IMF to decompose non-stationary EEG into modes

frameworks.name: EEG-based affective computing
frameworks.one_line: brain-signal-driven recognition of discrete emotional states

core_question: Can an automated EEG-based pipeline using EMD/IMF and VMD effectively recognize human emotional states?
key_assumptions: brain signals carry separable information about emotional states
non-stationary EEG benefits from mode-decomposition before feature extraction
classifier performance depends on feature-extraction and feature-selection stages
manipulations: choice of signal-decomposition method (EMD/IMF vs VMD)
combination of feature extraction with feature selection and classification stages
key_findings: brain signals can be used to classify multiple emotional states
EEG signal instability makes emotion recognition difficult
pipeline combining EMD/IMF and VMD is proposed for EEG handling and feature extraction
interpretations: emotion recognition performance hinges on the efficiency of feature extraction, selection, and classification
combining EMD/IMF with VMD offers an effective EEG processing approach distinct from prior work
limitations_noted: brain signals are not stable, complicating recognition
